title: Open Redirect
owasp: CWE-601 – URL Redirection to Untrusted Site
summary: A "return to" link redirects to a user-supplied URL — meant for pages on this site. Each stage adds a stronger but still-bypassable check, until same-site-only validation.
goal: The /go?url= link is meant to send you back to a page on this site. Get it to redirect to an external site instead (an open redirect — the basis of a convincing phishing link).
goalSecure: Try the earlier payloads — the redirect should refuse to leave the site (falls back to /), while a real same-site path like /account still works.
rootCause: A redirect destination is taken from user input and used without verifying it points to an allowed (same-site) location, so an attacker can craft a link on the trusted site that bounces victims to a site they control.
lessons: Never redirect to a raw user-supplied URL — validate the destination against an allowlist or restrict it to same-site relative paths.
Blocking the scheme isn't enough: protocol-relative URLs (//evil.com) and other forms still leave the site.
Substring / prefix matching on the host is bypassable (trusted.com.evil.com, evil.com/?x=trusted.com) — parse the URL and compare the host exactly.
Prefer relative paths you build yourself, or map an opaque key to a known destination, over reflecting attacker URLs.

stages.title: Must start with our site URL
stages.defense: URL must start with the site's base URL.

stages.title: Require a leading-slash path
stages.defense: Must start with "/" and not be "//".

stages.title: Same-site relative paths only
stages.defense: Only same-site relative paths are allowed.
